missing_from_site_plan.design_brief_reference: Method Of Supply Requirement, Page 2 (sub-step-extract-hv and sub-step-extract-lv)
missing_from_site_plan.note: Compliance standards and mandatory codes are not represented in the Site Plan legend data. These are regulatory requirements referenced in the Design Brief with no corresponding drawing symbol or legend entry.

method_of_supply_comparison.summary: The Design Brief specifies 12 distinct method of supply requirements and work items spanning HV works, LV works, SL works, service connections, duct provisions, and associated funding obligations. The Site Plan legend provides partial representation for overhead cable stringing, conductor removal, pole-related works, HV and LV trenching, duct infrastructure, and substation assets through its symbol set. However, significant gaps exist: specific pole reference numbers and route locations cannot be verified from legend data alone; no dedicated SL removal or installation symbols are present beyond generic lantern entries; the new OH road crossing service connection has no legend representation; spare HV duct designation is not explicitly shown; and all commercial, financial, and compliance standard items from the Design Brief have no corresponding Site Plan legend entries. Overall, the Site Plan legend supports the general nature of the works but lacks the granular asset-level, location-specific, and commercial detail required to fully validate all Design Brief method of supply requirements.

missing_from_drawing.item: Spare HV duct as per Fact Sheet 11 (Endeavour Energy capital contribution)
missing_from_drawing.note: No specific drawing symbol or legend entry explicitly identifies a 'spare HV duct' or references Fact Sheet 11. While 'EXISTING DUCTS' and 'NEW HV TRENCH' are present in the legend, neither distinctly represents a spare duct allocation as a funded capital contribution item. The spare duct scope is not visually distinguishable in the drawing legend.

missing_from_drawing.item: Duct usage charge as required (Customer reimbursement to Endeavour Energy)
missing_from_drawing.note: No drawing symbol or legend entry represents a duct usage charge. This is a financial/administrative obligation with no corresponding visual depiction in the site plan or legend.

No legend entry or drawing symbol specifically represents network switching operations. While HV ABS (N/C) and HV USL (N/C) appear in the legend as switching-related equipment, no switching work action symbol (e.g. 'OPERATE SWITCH' or similar) is present. The funded switching activity is not explicitly scoped in the drawing.

missing_from_drawing.design_brief_reference: Determination of Funding, page 4; Method of Supply Requirement, page 2
missing_from_drawing.note: Substation commissioning is identified as a funded non-contestable work in the Design Brief, but no corresponding substation element (e.g. PADMOUNT SUBSTATION or POLE SUBSTATION symbols) appears to be specifically called out in the site plan data as being commissioned or installed. The legend contains PADMOUNT SUBSTATION and POLE SUBSTATION symbols, but no 'NEW SUBSTATION' or 'COMMISSION SUBSTATION' action legend entry exists to confirm this scope is depicted.
Contract inspection is an administrative/procedural funded work item with no corresponding drawing symbol or legend entry. It cannot be visually represented or verified in the site plan.

missing_from_drawing.item: All other works required (Customer funded contestable works)
missing_from_drawing.note: This is an open-ended catch-all funding category with no specific scope definition in the Design Brief. Without further detail, it is not possible to verify whether all implied works are depicted in the drawing. The vague nature of this item means potential works may be unaccounted for in the drawing.

missing_from_drawing.item: New OH road crossing service connection (SL/LV works)
missing_from_drawing.note: A new OH road crossing service connection is described in the LV works section, but no specific legend entry for 'ROAD CROSSING' or 'SERVICE CONNECTION' exists in the drawing legend. The closest applicable symbol is 'STRING NEW OH CABLE', but this does not explicitly identify a road crossing service connection as a distinct funded scope item.

funding_arrangement_comparison.summary: Of the 11 distinct funded work items identified in the Design Brief, approximately 4 have reasonably corresponding legend entries or drawing symbols in the site plan (removal of OH conductors, stringing of new OH cables, HV trench works, LV trench works). However, 7 items are either missing from or not explicitly represented in the drawing: the spare HV duct capital contribution, the duct usage charge, network switching operations, substation commissioning, contract inspection, the catch-all contestable works category, and the new OH road crossing service connection. The drawing legend lacks action-specific symbols for commissioning, switching, inspection, and service crossing activities, and no symbol distinguishes a 'spare' duct from existing duct infrastructure. These gaps indicate that the drawing does not fully account for the complete funding arrangement scope described in the Design Brief.
